key_points: Campus housing is relatively new — UNO historically had none
Move-in videos exist but student commentary on dorms is sparse
Lack of on-campus housing was a dealbreaker for some past students
key_quotes: I grew up when UNO had no campus housing. One reason why I didn't go. I wanted to stay on a campus.
Went to school in Carolina so I didn't fare too bad.

key_points: Commuter-school culture limits traditional campus social scene
Omaha city itself offers social outlets, but campus life feels thin
No strong evidence of active clubs, Greek life, or events from students
key_quotes: I'm missing out on the social aspect of college by being on my own.
Wanna grab a beer? Or smoke a bowl? — the Omaha social scene is informal and local-driven.

key_points: No student comments about campus dining in either data source
Commuter-heavy population may mean dining halls are underused
Zero specific praise or complaints — a blind spot in available data
key_quotes: No direct student quotes about UNO dining were available in the data.
Commuter school nature suggests many students eat off-campus regularly.

key_points: One student described a multi-year academic struggle with real emotional toll
Feeling 'too dumb' after repeated course failures reflects support gaps
No mentions of counseling services or mental health resources from students
key_quotes: I'm in my 5th (and hopefully final) year at UNO. I'm on my third attempt at passing College Algebra.
I study from the practice homework and tests... but I have failed 1/2 of the tests required.

key_points: UNO is chosen primarily for affordability, not prestige or aid packages
Students transfer to or from UNO mainly based on cost comparisons
No reports of generous merit scholarships or need-based aid from students
key_quotes: Comparing tuition to other colleges close to me, it's cheaper.
MSSU has in-state tuition for Nebraska residents — so some leave UNO for cheaper options.

key_points: Math Lab is the primary academic support — but students find it unusable
No tutoring offered for College Algebra despite being a gateway course
CS program is mentioned as a realistic option, but academic rigor is questioned
key_quotes: My school doesn't offer tutoring for College Algebra students; those who need guidance should go to the Math Lab. I can't focus in the Math Lab.
I'm in my 5th (and hopefully final) year at UNO... on my third attempt at passing College Algebra.

key_points: Admissions slow to respond to international student inquiries
No proactive communication noted — students navigate processes alone
Bureaucratic friction reported even for straightforward application questions
key_quotes: I sent an email to UNO admissions about the same, but have not heard anything yet.
Have you looked over their instructions for international students? — peer had to step in where admin didn't.

key_points: Omaha is affordable but raises diversity and inclusion concerns
City described as friendly but with pockets that are 'the worst types of humans'
UNO is urban-adjacent but lacks a true residential campus feel
key_quotes: Honestly, it depends on where in Omaha. There's random areas where people are just the worst types of humans for some reason.
Is Omaha diverse? How friendly are they to people of color — more specifically, black people?

key_points: Finance and investment speakers visit campus — notable for business students
Mohnish Pabrai held a session at UNO, suggesting some industry engagement
No student testimonials about job placement, internships, or career services
key_quotes: Mohnish Pabrai's Session at The University of Nebraska, Omaha — a rare high-profile industry event.
He is a truly genuine, calm and humble guy. Lots to learn from such beings.

key_points: UNO is chosen as a budget option, not for quality or outcomes
Students spending 5+ years to finish due to course failures raises cost concerns
Cheaper than many alternatives but value depends heavily on program and support
key_quotes: I'm in my 5th (and hopefully final) year at the University of Nebraska Omaha.
Comparing tuition to other colleges close to me, it's cheaper — that's the primary draw.

key_points: UNO feels like a practical, no-frills commuter school — not a campus experience
Students end up here by default, not by choice — low enthusiasm in posts
Decent for local students watching costs; poor fit for those wanting campus life
key_quotes: I grew up when UNO had no campus housing. One reason why I didn't go.
I'm missing out on the social aspect of college by being on my own.

key_points: **Academics:** No tutoring for College Algebra; Math Lab is the only option and students find it unusable
**Retention:** One student took 5 years and 3 attempts at a single required course with minimal institutional support
**Diversity & Safety:** Multiple prospective students flagged concerns about racial inclusivity in Omaha broadly
key_quotes: My school doesn't offer tutoring for College Algebra students... I can't focus in the Math Lab.
Is Omaha diverse? How friendly are they to people of color — more specifically, black people?

key_points: High-profile finance speakers like Mohnish Pabrai visit — rare for a mid-tier school
New campus housing opens doors for students who previously had no residential option
Omaha's low cost of living reduces overall student debt burden significantly
key_quotes: I grew up when UNO had no campus housing — implying recent housing expansion is a genuine improvement.
Comparing tuition to other colleges close to me, it's cheaper — and Omaha living costs back that up.
